metadata.title: ChoiceList
metadata.description: Present multiple options to users, allowing either single selections with radio buttons or multiple selections with checkboxes.

properties.description: Additional text to provide context or guidance for the field. This text is displayed along with the field and its label to offer more information or instructions to the user. This will also be exposed to screen reader users.
Disables the field, disallowing any interaction. disabled on any child choices is ignored when this is true.
Indicate an error to the user. The field will be given a specific stylistic treatment to communicate problems that have to be resolved immediately.
Content to use as the field label.
Changes the visibility of the component's label. visible: the label is visible to all users.exclusive: the label is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.
Whether multiple choices can be selected.
An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.
An array of the values of the selected options. This is a convenience prop for setting the selected prop on child options.

examples.name: Main example
examples.html_code:
<script>
  const handleChange = (event) =>
    console.log('Values: ', event.currentTarget.values);
</script>
<s-choice-list
  label="Company name"
  name="Company name"
  details="The company name will be displayed on the checkout page."
  onChange="handleChange(event)"
>
  <s-choice value="hidden">Hidden</s-choice>
  <s-choice value="optional">Optional</s-choice>
  <s-choice value="required">Required</s-choice>
</s-choice-list>
examples.erb_code:
<script>
  const handleChange = (event) =>
    console.log('Values: ', event.currentTarget.values);
</script>
<%= form.choice_list :company_name, label: "Company name", details: "The company name will be displayed on the checkout page.", onchange: "handleChange(event)" do %>
  <s-choice value="hidden">Hidden</s-choice>
  <s-choice value="optional">Optional</s-choice>
  <s-choice value="required">Required</s-choice>
<% end %>

examples.name: Basic usage
examples.description: Demonstrates a basic ChoiceList with single selection, showing how to create a group of radio button choices.
examples.html_code:
<s-choice-list label="Product visibility" name="visibility">
  <s-choice value="hidden">Hidden</s-choice>
  <s-choice value="optional">Optional</s-choice>
  <s-choice value="required" selected>Required</s-choice>
</s-choice-list>
examples.erb_code:
<%= form.choice_list :visibility, label: "Product visibility" do %>
  <s-choice value="hidden">Hidden</s-choice>
  <s-choice value="optional">Optional</s-choice>
  <s-choice value="required" selected="selected">Required</s-choice>
<% end %>

examples.name: Multiple selections
examples.description: Illustrates a ChoiceList with multiple selection enabled, allowing users to choose multiple options with additional descriptive details for each choice.
examples.html_code:
<s-choice-list label="Checkout options" name="checkout" multiple>
  <s-choice value="shipping" selected>
    Use the shipping address as the billing address by default
    <s-text slot="details">
      Reduces the number of fields required to check out. The billing address
      can still be edited.
    </s-text>
  </s-choice>
  <s-choice value="confirmation">
    Require a confirmation step
    <s-text slot="details">
      Customers must review their order details before purchasing.
    </s-text>
  </s-choice>
</s-choice-list>
examples.erb_code:
<%= form.choice_list :checkout, label: "Checkout options", multiple: true do %>
  <s-choice value="shipping" selected="selected">
    Use the shipping address as the billing address by default
    <s-text slot="details">
      Reduces the number of fields required to check out. The billing address
      can still be edited.
    </s-text>
  </s-choice>
  <s-choice value="confirmation">
    Require a confirmation step
    <s-text slot="details">
      Customers must review their order details before purchasing.
    </s-text>
  </s-choice>
<% end %>

examples.name: With error state
examples.description: Shows how to display an error message in a ChoiceList when an invalid selection is made or a validation constraint is not met.
examples.html_code:
<s-choice-list
  label="Product visibility"
  name="visibility"
  error="Product visibility cannot be hidden at this time"
>
  <s-choice value="hidden">Hidden</s-choice>
  <s-choice value="optional">Optional</s-choice>
  <s-choice value="required" selected>Required</s-choice>
</s-choice-list>
examples.erb_code:
<%= form.choice_list :visibility, label: "Product visibility", error: "Product visibility cannot be hidden at this time" do %>
  <s-choice value="hidden">Hidden</s-choice>
  <s-choice value="optional">Optional</s-choice>
  <s-choice value="required" selected="selected">Required</s-choice>
<% end %>

examples.name: Multiple choices with details
examples.description: Showcases a multiple-selection ChoiceList with each option including detailed information.
examples.html_code:
<s-choice-list
  label="Available shipping methods"
  name="shipping-methods"
  multiple
>
  <s-choice value="standard" selected>
    Standard shipping
    <s-text slot="details">5-7 business days delivery</s-text>
  </s-choice>
  <s-choice value="express" selected>
    Express shipping
    <s-text slot="details">2-3 business days delivery</s-text>
  </s-choice>
  <s-choice value="overnight">
    Overnight shipping
    <s-text slot="details">Next business day delivery</s-text>
  </s-choice>
</s-choice-list>
examples.erb_code:
<%= form.choice_list :shipping_methods, label: "Available shipping methods", multiple: true do %>
  <s-choice value="standard" selected="selected">
    Standard shipping
    <s-text slot="details">5-7 business days delivery</s-text>
  </s-choice>
  <s-choice value="express" selected="selected">
    Express shipping
    <s-text slot="details">2-3 business days delivery</s-text>
  </s-choice>
  <s-choice value="overnight">
    Overnight shipping
    <s-text slot="details">Next business day delivery</s-text>
  </s-choice>
<% end %>

examples.name: Choice list validation
examples.description: Interactive example showing required choice validation with dynamic error messages.
